evals.prompt: I just modified docker_orchestrator.py in the visibility_service and want to make sure the code quality is good before I commit. Can you audit my changes with SonarQube?
evals.expected_output: Analysis of uncommitted changes in docker_orchestrator.py, showing any SonarQube issues found in the modified lines with severity, type, line numbers, and recommendations

assertions.name: Uses git diff to identify uncommitted changes
assertions.description: Should run 'git diff --name-only HEAD' or similar to find changed files

assertions.name: Resolves SonarQube project key
assertions.description: Should search for project key in config files or use search_my_sonarqube_projects

assertions.name: Calls analyze_code_snippet with correct parameters
assertions.description: Should call mcp__sonarqube__analyze_code_snippet with projectKey, filePath, language, and codeSnippet

assertions.name: Extracts changed code from diff
assertions.description: Should get the actual diff output and extract added/modified lines

assertions.name: Detects Python language correctly
assertions.description: Should identify docker_orchestrator.py as Python and pass language=['python']

assertions.name: Produces structured audit report
assertions.description: Output should include severity breakdown and issues grouped by file with line numbers

assertions.name: Saves audit report to outputs directory
assertions.description: Should create audit_report.md in the specified outputs directory

evals.prompt: I've staged some changes for commit in the visibility_service. Before I push, run a SonarQube audit on them to make sure there are no security issues or code smells.
evals.expected_output: Analysis of staged changes only, filtering for security issues and code quality problems, with detailed breakdown by file and severity

assertions.name: Uses git diff --cached for staged changes
assertions.description: Should run 'git diff --cached --name-only' or 'git diff --staged' to find staged files

assertions.name: Resolves SonarQube project key
assertions.description: Should find the project key for visibility_service

assertions.name: Calls analyze_code_snippet for each staged file
assertions.description: Should call mcp__sonarqube__analyze_code_snippet for all staged files with appropriate parameters

assertions.name: Highlights security issues in output
assertions.description: Should clearly indicate security-related issues (VULNERABILITY, SECURITY_HOTSPOT, or SECURITY impact)

assertions.name: Groups results by file
assertions.description: Output should organize issues by file with clear file headers

assertions.name: Includes severity breakdown
assertions.description: Should provide a summary section showing count of issues by severity level

assertions.name: Saves audit report to outputs directory
assertions.description: Should create audit_report.md in the specified outputs directory

evals.prompt: I'm about to create an MR for this branch. Can you check all the code I've changed since branching from develop? Just want to make sure I'm not introducing any blocker or high severity issues.
evals.expected_output: Analysis of all branch changes compared to develop branch, showing issues grouped by file with focus on BLOCKER and HIGH severity problems

assertions.name: Compares branch to develop
assertions.description: Should run 'git diff --name-only develop...HEAD' or similar to find all branch changes

assertions.name: Resolves SonarQube project key
assertions.description: Should find the project key for the repository

assertions.name: Analyzes all changed files in branch
assertions.description: Should call mcp__sonarqube__analyze_code_snippet for each file changed in the branch

assertions.name: Focuses on BLOCKER and HIGH severity
assertions.description: Should clearly highlight or prioritize BLOCKER and HIGH severity issues in the output

assertions.name: Handles multiple files correctly
assertions.description: Should process multiple changed files and present results for all of them

assertions.name: Provides severity summary
assertions.description: Should include a summary showing total issues by severity, making BLOCKER/HIGH visible upfront

assertions.name: Saves audit report to outputs directory
assertions.description: Should create audit_report.md in the specified outputs directory
